original_query: SELECT "t4"."NAME" FROM (SELECT "NAME", "DEPTNO", "DEPTNO" - 10 AS "DEPTNOMINUS" FROM "DEPT") AS "t4" INNER JOIN (SELECT "DEPTNO", "SAL" + 1 AS "f9" FROM "EMP" GROUP BY "DEPTNO", "SAL" + 1) AS "t6" ON "t4"."DEPTNOMINUS" = "t6"."f9" AND "t4"."DEPTNO" = "t6"."DEPTNO"
rewritten_query:
SELECT "DEPT"."NAME"
FROM "DEPT"
JOIN (
    SELECT "DEPTNO"
    FROM "EMP"
    WHERE "SAL" = "DEPTNO" - 11
) AS "t6" ON "DEPT"."DEPTNO" = "t6"."DEPTNO";

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Removed unnecessary GROUP BY in the subquery t6 and replaced it with a WHERE clause that filters EMP rows early
Eliminated redundant join condition since the WHERE clause ensures the necessary relationship
Simplified the query by removing unnecessary subqueries and columns
Pushed filter condition SAL = DEPTNO - 11 into the subquery to reduce the number of rows processed
